name: Memphis Slim
description: American recording artist; blues pianist, singer, and composer (1915–1988)
abstract: John Len Chatman (September 3, 1915 – February 24, 1988), known professionally as Memphis Slim, was an American blues pianist, singer, and composer. He led a series of bands that, reflecting the popular appeal of jump blues, included saxophones, bass, drums, and piano.

concerts.label: The Highlander Folk School Benefit Concert

Every Day I Have The Blues

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Ousley
about_quotes.evidence: In fact I worked with Sunnyland and Memphis Slim, Roosevelt Sykes.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer mentions Memphis Slim in relation to Jess Dixon; Interviewee confirms Dixon is big in Chicago but does not mention any personal connection to Memphis Slim.

about_quotes.speaker: Nat Hentoff
about_quotes.evidence: I just did a piece for the Wall Street Journal on a 46-year-old guy named Barrelhouse Chuck Goering, who ... learned at the hands of Sunnyland Slim and Memphis Slim

about_quotes.speaker: Tommy Ridgley
about_quotes.evidence: He said, well let's think of something. And one name always stick in my mind. He said, let's come with something like Memphis Slim or something.

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: when I was growing up, because that’s when people like Memphis Slim and Muddy Waters were still living there in Chicago.
